attacking_style: Long-range solo attacks from 30-50km out, particularly effective when launched after group has been whittled down. Prefers calculated late-race moves rather than early opportunistic breaks.
team_tactics: Deployed as dual-purpose rider: strong diesel domestique for GC leaders on climbs, or insurance policy in early breaks when team has GC ambitions. Capable of solo chase work matching WorldTour team outputs.
terrain_preference: Mountain stages with extended final climbs where attrition plays out. Shows superior thermoregulation in hot conditions. Struggles on punchy final climbs when pace is explosive rather than steady.
vulnerability: Gets distanced on sharp, explosive final climbs when protecting GC position. Less effective in one-day Monument scenarios where positioning requires constant high intensity rather than steady diesel work.
key_patterns: Executes long solo attacks 37-50km from finish, learning from failures to refine timing (2021 TdF evidence)
Functions as GC insurance in early breaks, positioned 4-5 minutes back as tactical safety net
Performs extended solo chase work matching team outputs, diesel engine strength for summit finishes
Superior performance in heat/attrition scenarios; podiumed via thermoregulation advantage Tour de Suisse 2019
Top-10 placements in Monument support role; consistent positioning but not explosive enough for wins
Gets dropped on punchy final climbs when pace turns explosive rather than tempo-controlled
form_trajectory: neutral: Recent 2023 deployment in breakaway insurance roles suggests shift from stage-hunting to pure domestique duties, though 2022 top-10 finishes showed climbing strength remains intact.
